name: Round Flat Arabic Bread ("Pita" Bread)
recipeIngredient: 5 cups lukewarm water (for more nutritious breads, add 1 1/2 cups nonfat dry milk to water and beat well)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in 1 cup lukewarm water for 5 to 10 minutes.
2. Mix 15 cups flour and salt together in a large bowl, reserving 1 cup flour for kneading.
3. Add the dissolved yeast mixture and 5 cups lukewarm water (plus olive oil) all at once to the flour mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon or mix by hand until dough forms.
4. Turn dough out onto a floured board or cloth and knead very well, adding reserved flour gradually until the dough is elastic and no longer sticky.
5. Cover dough with a dry cloth and let it rise in a warm place until doubled in size (about 2 hours).
6. Punch down the dough and divide into 25 to 30 balls, about the size of small oranges.
7. Cover dough balls with a dry cloth and let them rise for another 30 minutes.
8. Roll each ball into a 7 to 8-inch circle, no more than 1/4 inch thick.
9. Place the circles on floured boards or a table. Let them rise covered until doubled in size (about 1 hour).
10. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C).
11. Place bread rounds onto baking sheets, 2 per sheet. When transferring the dough rounds to the baking sheets, flip them so the top side becomes the bottom side.
12. Bake for 4 to 6 minutes in the preheated oven, then check after 2 to 3 minutes. The breads will puff up on the baking sheets and brown slightly on the bottom.
13. Slide the baked breads onto a board and lightly spray with water. Cover with a damp cloth until cool enough to handle.
14. When completely cooled, gently flatten the breads with your hand and stack them covered with a damp tea towel for 2 to 3 hours.
Freezes well.
